# FERC Audit Explorer

> Machine-readable findings of noncompliance and staff recommendations mined from **utility audit and prudence records**, across three collections: **FERC audits** (the Office of Enforcement's financial (FA) and non-financial (PA) audits of utilities, ISOs/RTOs, and pipelines, electric/gas/oil), **FERC prudence reviews** (rate-case cost determinations), and **state PUC/PSC/SCC audits** (rate, fuel-cost, and management proceedings from several state commissions). FERC audits and the parsed state management audits are broken into findings -> recommendations (verbatim); legal orders, testimony, and settlements are listed with their source. Themes are mined by transparent keyword tagging, never LLM judgement.

Scope: FERC utility audits — electric, gas & oil, extended to prudence reviews and state audits. As of 2026-06-22, the corpus holds 660 structured records: 123 FERC audits (of 123 listed at https://www.ferc.gov/audits (261 electric, 49 gas, 18 oil, 2 water identified)), 29 prudence reviews, and 387 state PUC audits. Findings and recommendations are quoted verbatim. Independent public-interest tool, not affiliated with FERC. Links below are relative to the site root.

## Data (machine-readable)
- [Reports](data/reports.json): every structured record with findings, recommendations, and themes
- [Patterns](data/patterns.json): cross-report themes with counts by industry and year
- [Patterns by collection](data/patterns_by_collection.json): the same aggregates, per collection/tab
- [Meta](data/meta.json): corpus counts and provenance
- [Full corpus text](llms-full.txt): all findings, recommendations, and source notes in one file (verbatim)
- [LLM context](llms-ctx.txt): the same corpus in the llms.txt XML context shape (`<project>`/`<doc>`)

## FERC Audits
_123 document(s), 2014–2026. 596 findings / 1424 recommendations parsed from 123 report(s). top themes: Accounting misclassification, Form reporting (Form No. 1/2/6, Page 700), Depreciation, Property & plant records._

- [Southern Electric Generating Company](https://elibrary.ferc.gov/eLibrary/filelist?accession_number=20260507-3001&optimized=false): Docket FA24-3-000, issued 2026-05-07, financial audit, generation, 4 finding(s) — findings: Accounting for Replacement of Minor Items of Property; Allowance for Funds Used During Construction; Computation of Depreciation Rates; FERC Form Nos. 1 and 3-Q Reporting

## Prudence Reviews
_29 document(s), 2013–2025. jurisdictions: FERC (14), WA (3), GA (3), WI (2), CA (2), IL (2), IN (1), VA (1), KS (1). 29 listed for reference (orders / testimony / settlements). doc types: ALJ initial decision (5), Commission order on formal challenge (3), fuel cost reconciliation prudence review — Final Decision (2), ERRA compliance & reasonableness review decision (2), Vogtle construction-monitoring report (semi-annual cost/schedule prudence monitoring) (2), order on Schedule 95 Power Cost Adjustment Clause revisions (power-cost prudence/rate order) (1), gas cost adjustment (GCA) order — gas-cost prudence/recovery review (1), power cost adjustment (PCA) prudence-review order (1), fuel factor order (fuel-cost recovery review) (1), Commission order on rehearing (1), Commission order on wholesale power contracts (1), gas cost (PGA) reconciliation order (1), Commission order on complaint (1), fuel & purchased-power prudence staff audit report (1), Commission order on remand (1), PGA / gas-adjustment-charge reconciliation prudence order (§9-220) (1), policy & interpretive statement on LDC natural-gas hedging practices (compliance/prudence review) (1), Commission opinion on initial decision (1), Commission order on abandonment cost recovery (1), PSC Staff & Independent Construction Monitor prudence testimony (Vogtle 22nd VCM report) (1). top themes: Fuel & purchased-power cost recovery, Cost of service & rates, Below-the-line costs (lobbying, charitable, etc.)._

- [Puget Sound Energy](https://www.utc.wa.gov/casedocket/2025/250747/docsets): WA · order on Schedule 95 Power Cost Adjustment Clause revisions (power-cost prudence/rate order), Docket UE-250747, issued 2025-12-24 — listed for reference
- [Southern Indiana Gas and Electric Company d/b/a CenterPoint Energy Indiana South](https://www.in.gov/oucc/natural-gas/tips-and-publications/gas-cost-adjustments/): IN · gas cost adjustment (GCA) order — gas-cost prudence/recovery review, Docket Cause No. 37366 GCA 168, issued 2025-10-29 — listed for reference
- [Puget Sound Energy](https://www.utc.wa.gov/casedocket/2025/250318/docsets): WA · power cost adjustment (PCA) prudence-review order, Docket UE-250318, issued 2025-09-29 — listed for reference
- [ISO New England Inc. et al.](https://elibrary.ferc.gov/eLibrary/filelist?accession_number=20250918-3062&optimized=false): Commission order on formal challenge, Docket ER20-2054, issued 2025-09-18 — listed for reference
- [Wisconsin Power and Light Company (Alliant Energy)](https://apps.psc.wi.gov/ERF/ERFsearch/content/documentInfo.aspx?docid=559688): WI · fuel cost reconciliation prudence review — Final Decision, Docket 6680-FR-2024, issued 2025-08-28 — listed for reference
- [Wisconsin Electric Power Company (We Energies)](https://apps.psc.wi.gov/ERF/ERFsearch/content/documentInfo.aspx?docid=557994): WI · fuel cost reconciliation prudence review — Final Decision, Docket 6630-FR-2024, issued 2025-08-12 — listed for reference

## State PUC Audits
_387 document(s), 2017–2025. jurisdictions: WA (111), IN (55), PA (53), DC (33), TX (30), NY (10), WV (10), ME (10), MI (6), MT (6), AK (6), TN (5), NJ (5), ID (5), OK (4), MS (3), MD (3), CA (3), IL (3), IA (3), CT (3), UT (2), OH (2), KY (2), GA (2), MO (2), AR (2), WI (1), SC (1), OR (1), NV (1), NM (1), ND (1), HI (1), AZ (1). 587 findings / 761 recommendations parsed from 17 report(s). 370 listed for reference (orders / testimony / settlements). doc types: report (143), — (138), audit/report (26), internal audit (19), filing/report (11), management efficiency investigation (3), utilities staff annual report (2), energy balancing account audit report (2), distribution reliability audit — Part Two (programs & operations) (2), distribution reliability audit — Part One (infrastructure) (2), comprehensive management & operations audit (PSC order releasing audit report, PSL 66(19)) (2), annual report (2), staff prudence review report (2), management and operations audit (2), weather normalization adjustment audit (1), comprehensive management & operations audit implementation plans (utility response to audit recommendations) (1), comprehensive management & operations audit (PSC order releasing audit report, PSL §66(19)) (1), order initiating gas-system safety-inspection investigation (documents PSC Engineering Division audit findings) (1), comprehensive management & operations audit final report (independent auditor, PSL §66(19)) (1), comprehensive management & operations audit final report (independent auditor, LIPA Reform Act) (1), comprehensive management & operations audit (PSC order initiating audit, PSL §66(19)) (1), rider audit report (Daymark Energy Advisors, PUCO third-party audit of Rider DMR) (1), independent investigative audit (Blue Ridge Consulting, PUCO-ordered) (1), ICC investigation order incorporating Liberty management audit (gas System Modernization Program) (1), performance investigation final decision (1), rate design investigation decision (1), audit report (1), DPS Office of Investigations & Enforcement investigation report (customer billing system) (1). top themes: Internal audit & internal controls, Customer service & billing, Affiliate / intercompany transactions, Workforce, training & succession planning._

## Common themes (from parsed findings)
- Accounting misclassification — Costs or revenues booked to the wrong FERC account. (68 report(s), 182 finding(s))
- Form reporting (Form No. 1/2/6, Page 700) — Errors or omissions in the annual FERC forms utilities file. (58 report(s), 155 finding(s))
- Fuel & purchased-power cost recovery — Fuel, purchased-power, and energy-cost recovery or prudence matters. (54 report(s), 28 finding(s))
- Cost of service & rates — Errors in rate-base or return inputs to cost-of-service rates. (47 report(s), 85 finding(s))
- Depreciation — Unapproved or incorrect depreciation rates applied to plant. (47 report(s), 68 finding(s))
- Below-the-line costs (lobbying, charitable, etc.) — Non-recoverable costs (lobbying, charity, ads) charged to ratepayers. (43 report(s), 51 finding(s))
- Property & plant records — Incomplete or inaccurate utility plant and property records. (42 report(s), 76 finding(s))
- Affiliate / intercompany transactions — Transactions with affiliated companies mis-priced or mis-reported. (40 report(s), 145 finding(s))
- AFUDC / cost of capital — Mis-stated AFUDC — the financing cost capitalized during construction. (40 report(s), 53 finding(s))
- Tariff administration & oversight — Not following the utility's own FERC-approved tariff. (37 report(s), 63 finding(s))
- Internal audit & internal controls — Weak internal controls, compliance programs, or internal-audit coverage. (32 report(s), 32 finding(s))
- Capitalization vs. expense — Costs capitalized that should be expensed, or the reverse. (21 report(s), 22 finding(s))
- Service reliability & vegetation management — Electric reliability (SAIDI/CAIDI), outages, or vegetation-management programs. (15 report(s), 35 finding(s))
- Customer service & billing — Customer-service performance, billing accuracy, or call-center issues. (14 report(s), 28 finding(s))
- Storm cost recovery & securitization — Storm restoration costs, storm riders/reserves, or securitization. (13 report(s), 29 finding(s))
- Inventory, materials & fleet — Inventory accuracy, materials management, or fleet/vehicle management gaps. (13 report(s), 20 finding(s))
- Workforce, training & succession planning — Staffing, training, span-of-control, or leadership-succession shortcomings. (12 report(s), 34 finding(s))
- Emergency preparedness & business continuity — Weak emergency response, storm readiness, or business-continuity planning. (11 report(s), 14 finding(s))
- Membership dues & industry associations — Trade-association dues (e.g., EEI) improperly charged to ratepayers. (11 report(s), 11 finding(s))
- Information technology & systems — IT governance, systems, budgets, or project-management deficiencies. (10 report(s), 14 finding(s))
- Informational postings — Required public postings (e.g., OASIS) missing, late, or incomplete. (9 report(s), 15 finding(s))
- Cybersecurity & physical security — Gaps in cyber defenses or physical security of facilities and systems. (8 report(s), 20 finding(s))
- Dividend policy & capital management — Dividend-policy or capital-management practices flagged by auditors. (6 report(s), 8 finding(s))
- Gas safety & pipeline integrity — Gas leak backlogs, corrosion control, or pipeline-integrity practices. (3 report(s), 3 finding(s))
- Creditworthiness — Customer credit standards not applied as the tariff requires. (2 report(s), 2 finding(s))

## Optional
- [Human-readable explorer](index.html)
- [FERC audit reports (primary source)](https://www.ferc.gov/audits)
